name: Victoria Spivey
abstract: Victoria Regina Spivey (October 15, 1906 – October 3, 1976), sometimes known as Queen Victoria, was an American blues singer, songwriter, and record company founder. During a recording career that spanned 40 years, from 1926 to the mid-1960s, she worked with  Louis Armstrong, King Oliver, Clarence Williams, Luis Russell, Lonnie Johnson, …

concerts.label: Concert by Big Joe Williams and Victoria Spivey

about_quotes.speaker: Freddie Moore
about_quotes.evidence: We made this Rag-and-Roll Revue... we worked together. We'd do things like... 'All Of Me.' ... She'd sing one chorus of it and I'd sing the second chorus

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Didn't we do something with this girl Victoria Spivey? Didn't you and I do something with her once? [INTERVIEWEE] Yes.

about_quotes.speaker: Sippie Wallace
about_quotes.evidence: She would always come from Brooklyn to visit me here... I know -- this Victoria, this Vicki. I ______ that girl.

about_quotes.speaker: Jo Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I go to Lonny Johnson in 1923 with Victoria Spivey and 'Furniture Man Blues'

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Tate
about_quotes.evidence: So I was with Victoria Spivey, she had a review... and we were on a tour
